commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And they departed from Abronah, and encamped at Eziongeber. Some place this station on the Mediterranean, where Strabo fixes the city of Gassion Gaber, the Beto Gabria of Ptolemy. But the Scripture informs us it lay on the Red Sea, 3 Kings ix. Cellarius thinks most probably upon the Ela nitic gulph, to the east of that of Suez, or Hero polis, where Josephus maintains Asiongaber or Bernice stood. The Hebrews came to this station from that of Elat, Deuteronomy ii.

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
